document: REVISED REGULATORY FRAMEWORK FOR BANK VERIFICATION NUMBER (BVN) OPERATIONS AND WATCH-LIST FOR THE NIGERIAN BANKING INDUSTRY 121021 FINAL (Nigeria - Row 11)

sections.summary: This section establishes the regulatory framework for Bank Verification Number (BVN) operations and Watch-list in Nigeria's banking industry. It defines the BVN system as a unique customer identifier deployed in 2014 to enhance KYC compliance and payment security. The framework outlines the roles of Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Nigeria Inter-Bank Settlement System (NIBSS), banks, other financial institutions, and customers in managing BVN data and watch-list processes to combat fraud and ensure system integrity. This section details access rules for the BVN database, restricting access to Tier 1 entities (banks, other financial institutions, mobile money operators) with direct access, while requiring CBN approval for Tier 2 entities. It prohibits foreign entities from accessing the BVN database and mandates strict confidentiality for all users. The section also specifies conditions for BVN information requests, restrictions on BVN use to prevent abuse, and security requirements including data storage within Nigeria and encryption. sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the BVN system and its operational scope, which is critical for DTIAs as it establishes the foundational data identifiers and participant responsibilities for cross-border data handling within Nigeria's banking sector. This section imposes critical restrictions on cross-border data access to the BVN database, requiring CBN approval for non-Tier 1 entities and prohibiting foreign entities from accessing Nigerian data. It mandates data residency within Nigeria and strict security protocols, directly impacting international data transfers and cross-border compliance. This sec

requirements.text: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has the regulatory and oversight function on the BVN system in Nigeria.
The CBN shall review and approve the regulatory Framework and the Standard Operating Guidelines.
The CBN shall approve eligible users for access to the BVN information.
The CBN shall ensure that the objectives of the BVN initiatives are fully achieved.
NIBSS shall collaborate with other stakeholders to develop and review the Standard Operating Guidelines of the BVN.
NIBSS shall ensure seamless operations of the BVN system.
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall ensure proper capturing of BVN data and validate same before linkage with customers' accounts/wallets (except Tier 1).
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall ensure all operated accounts/wallets (except Tier 1) are linked with signatories' BVNs within 24 hours of NIBSS making BVN available.
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall enroll all mobile money wallets (except Tier 1) subscribers on the BVN database and link their wallets within 180 days of the framework issuance.
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall ensure BVN details of signatories, directors, and beneficial owners are linked to non-individual accounts/wallets (except Tier 1).
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall ensure customer's name on the BVN database matches across all accounts/wallets (except Tier 1).
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall report BVNs of individuals in breach to NIBSS for Watch-list update within 1 business day of breach.
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall report BVNs of confirmed deceased customers to NIBSS for 'DECEASED' designation within 24 hours of confirmation.
Banks and Other Financial Institutions shall use the Watch-list report submitted by participants with clearance from CBN's Risk Management Department to delist BVNs from the Watch-list.
Customers shall provide accurate biometrics and biodata and report changes in biometrics or biodata.
Customers shall report all suspicious or unauthorized activities on their accounts/wallets (except Tier 1) to their banks/OFIs.
Access to the BVN database shall be approved by the CBN and guided in line with the grid for Tier 1 and Tier 2 users
Foreign entities shall not be allowed access to the BVN database
BVN data shall be stored within the shores of Nigeria and shall not be routed across borders without the consent of the CBN
Users of the BVN information shall establish adequate security procedures to ensure the safety and security of its information
Access to BVN information by customers shall be obtained through secured channels with appropriate authentication
BVN information shall be treated as confidential
All stakeholders shall comply with Nigeria Data Protection Regulation (NDPR) or any regulation of the Bank on data protection
Ensure proper capturing of the BVN data (including BVN captured by their agents) for all customers
Ensure validation and linkage of BVN data in line with the BVN Framework and BVN SOG
Ensure all operated accounts/wallets (except Tier 1) are linked with the signatories' BVN within 24 hours of NIBSS making BVN available
Ensure BVN details of all signatories, Directors, and Beneficial owners are linked to their respective non-individual accounts/wallets (except Tier 1)
Report the BVN of individuals in breach to NIBSS for update of the Watch-list
Report the BVN of confirmed deceased customers to NIBSS for designation as 'DECEASED' within 2 business days
Where a bank needs to watch-list a customer of another participating bank, the Chief Audit Executive of the customer's bank shall be notified
Upon notification, the Chief Audit Executive shall comply with the procedure for investigating and watch-listing the customer
Enforce sanctions on customers as stipulated
Use the BVN API only for account/wallet opening, maintenance and operational purposes and ensure full compliance with relevant data privacy laws

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank of Nigeria, in collaboration with the Bankers' Committee, deployed a centralised Bank Verification Number (BVN) System in February 2014.
